title: Bogotá three-body urban coordination
summary: Peñalosa's Bogotá transformation as a virtuous cycle: political vision enables public infrastructure, which reshapes citizen behavior, which validates and renews the political mandate — producing emergent urban safety and livability.
source: Ch04 — "The City That Learned to Coordinate"

nodes.label: Political Vision
nodes.description: Equity-first mayoral mandate and will to confront car lobbies

nodes.label: Public Infrastructure
nodes.description: TransMilenio BRT, bike lanes, parks, libraries, pedestrian space

nodes.label: Citizen Behavior
nodes.description: Transit use, cycling, public space occupation, Ciclovía

nodes.label: Urban Emergence
nodes.description: Homicide drop, commute gains, civic dignity, city 'soul'

edges.description: Vision funds and authorizes infrastructure build
Quality public space invites transit and pedestrian use
Infrastructure directly enables safer, faster movement
Reclaimed public life drives safety and cohesion

identifiability_note: No counterfactual Bogotá exists; mandate, build, and behavior co-evolve, so the vision→behavior effect cannot be isolated from confounding civic trends.
